theorem: representation-to-spectrum derivation of the 24-round repair count
claim: The selected screen-capacity repair cycle has m_rep=24.

branch.name: OPH realized observer-visible Standard Model product branch
conditions: no simple-GUT X/Y mixed gauge bosons

repair_grammar.name: spectrum-complete reversible adjoint repair grammar
repair_grammar.alphabet_rule: For each observer-visible compact-gauge adjoint generator a, include exactly two oriented primitive repair ticks a:+ and a:-.
repair_grammar.orientation_meaning: the two orientations are the action/coaction, write/verify, or ket/bra halves required by reversible overlap repair and record preservation on the OPH finite patch-carrier compare/write/verify slice
repair_grammar.tick_count_observable: rank of the active oriented adjoint support, equivalently the order of the cyclic scheduler on that support

spectral_object.name: cyclic repair clock on oriented adjoint support

derivation_chain.premise: OPH realized observer-visible product-gauge branch (corpus theorem)
uses: overlap-holonomy and zero-obstruction transport build a compact bosonic gauge category
DR/Tannaka reconstruction reads off a compact group from that category
MAR selects the realized one-generation/one-Higgs matter package
anomaly cancellation, Yukawa invariance, and the central kernel of the realized action fix the hypercharge lattice and the Z6 quotient
derivation_chain.source_artifact: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (realized compact-gauge branch theorem: overlap holonomy + Tannaka reconstruction + MAR + anomaly cancellation, lines 482-504 establishing connected adjoint (8,1,0)(+)(1,3,0)(+)(1,1,0) and no mixed X/Y carrier); paper/deriving_the_particle_zoo_from_observer_consistency.tex
derivation_chain.conclusion: The realized OPH compact-gauge branch is (SU(3)xSU(2)xU(1))/Z6 with connected adjoint (8,1,0)(+)(1,3,0)(+)(1,1,0); there are no mixed (3,2,+/-5/6) X/Y gauge bosons and no extra visible low-scale U(1) on this branch.

uses: dim(su(n)) = n^2 - 1 for compact SU(n)
dim(u(1)) = 1 for the abelian factor
derivation_chain.source_artifact: standard compact Lie algebra; encoded by su_dim(n)=n*n-1 in this verifier

uses: step 1 (realized product-gauge branch fixes the active adjoint sector)
step 2 (per-factor adjoint dimensions)
the unoriented adjoint dimension on the realized branch is the sum of per-factor adjoint dimensions
derivation_chain.source_artifact: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (line 301: the OPH screen branch supplies twelve curvature ports as the unoriented product-adjoint dimension)
derivation_chain.conclusion: unoriented product-adjoint repair register has dimension 8 + 3 + 1 = 12 (the OPH screen branch's twelve curvature ports)

uses: the OPH finite patch-carrier pipeline operates on a completed compare/write/verify slice
record-preserving repair requires every primitive repair tick to be paired with its co-oriented verification half (write/check, action/coaction, ket/bra)
for each unoriented adjoint generator a, the alphabet contains exactly two oriented primitive repair ticks a:+ and a:-
derivation_chain.source_artifact: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (reversible compare/write/verify slice, line 220; line 301-303: reversible write/check orientation doubles the product-adjoint repair register to 24)
derivation_chain.conclusion: orientation_multiplier = 2 (each unoriented adjoint generator contributes exactly two oriented primitive repair ticks)

derivation_chain.premise: Oriented adjoint support dimension on the realized branch
uses: step 3 (unoriented adjoint dimension = 12)
step 4 (orientation multiplier = 2)
the oriented support is the disjoint union of the two oriented primitives over each unoriented generator
derivation_chain.source_artifact: compact_proof_of_oph.tex line 301-303: 'Reversible write/check orientation doubles the product-adjoint repair register to 24'

uses: step 5 (24-dim oriented support)
the global repair cycle visits each oriented primitive exactly once
the induced linear shift C_rep: e_i -> e_{(i+1) mod m_rep} is a cyclic permutation of order m_rep on the oriented support
derivation_chain.source_artifact: C_rep is the cyclic permutation of the oriented adjoint basis; its eigenvalues are the m_rep-th roots of unity
derivation_chain.conclusion: spectral period of C_rep equals m_rep = 24; spectrum is {exp(2*pi*i*k/24): k=0,...,23}; tick-count observable equals m_rep = 24

derivation_chain.premise: Specialization of the parametric global repair-tick law
uses: step 5 (m_rep = 24)
the global repair-tick lemma establishes the parametric per-tick exponent law |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/(2*m)) for an m-tick homogeneous decomposition (R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json)
G_N = g_N^m_rep (the one-tick map is the positive homogeneous m_rep-th root of the full-cycle map)

derivation_chain.premise: Negative-control rejection of nearby round counts
uses: step 1 (no X/Y mixed gauge bosons; no simple-GUT carrier on the realized branch)
step 4 (orientation doubling required by reversible record-preserving repair)
the graviton is the dynamical metric branch, not an internal compact-gauge adjoint repair channel
derivation_chain.source_artifact: compact_proof_of_oph.tex line 503-504 (no X/Y leptoquark carrier on the realized product quotient); line 1098-1100 (unification without simple-GUT proton-decay channel)
derivation_chain.conclusion: every nearby round count (m=12 unoriented, m=6 minimal carrier, m=16 color-only doubled, m=22 without U(1), m=24 from single-orientation SU(5), m=48 from doubled SU(5), graviton-augmented support) is rejected on structural grounds; no other branch satisfying the OPH product-adjoint exclusion and orientation-doubling axioms yields a different round count

dim_su3_adjoint.role: color-adjoint dimension on the realized OPH product-gauge branch
dim_su3_adjoint.source_theorem: compact Lie algebra fact dim(su(n))=n^2-1 evaluated at n=3
dim_su3_adjoint.source_artifact: standard compact Lie algebra; encoded as su_dim(3)=8 in this verifier

dim_su2_adjoint.role: weak-isospin-adjoint dimension on the realized OPH product-gauge branch
dim_su2_adjoint.source_theorem: compact Lie algebra fact dim(su(n))=n^2-1 evaluated at n=2
dim_su2_adjoint.source_artifact: standard compact Lie algebra; encoded as su_dim(2)=3 in this verifier

dim_u1.role: hypercharge/abelian Lie algebra dimension on the realized branch
dim_u1.source_artifact: standard compact Lie algebra; encoded as 1 in this verifier

unoriented_total_twelve_curvature_ports.role: unoriented product-adjoint repair register dimension on the realized OPH branch
unoriented_total_twelve_curvature_ports.source_theorem: OPH twelve-curvature-port theorem on the realized product-gauge branch
unoriented_total_twelve_curvature_ports.source_artifact: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (line 301: the OPH screen branch supplies twelve curvature ports as the unoriented product-adjoint dimension)

orientation_multiplier.role: doubling from action/coaction (write/verify) reversible repair grammar
orientation_multiplier.source_theorem: OPH reversible orientation-doubling axiom on the patch-carrier compare/write/verify slice
orientation_multiplier.source_artifact: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (reversible compare/write/verify slice, line 220; line 301-303: reversible write/check orientation doubles the product-adjoint repair register to 24)

m_rep.role: global repair-tick round count; oriented support dimension equals cyclic-scheduler period
m_rep.source_theorem: representation-to-spectrum round-count theorem (this certificate, derivation_chain steps 1-6)

exponent_denominator.role: denominator of the per-tick screen-radius exponent -1/(2*m_rep) in |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/48)
exponent_denominator.source_theorem: specialization of the parametric global repair-tick law |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/(2*m)) at m=m_rep=24

branch_scope.oph_realized_compact_gauge_branch: the realized observer-visible compact-gauge branch with global group (SU(3) x SU(2) x U(1))/Z6, MAR-minimal one-Higgs sector, no extra visible low-scale U(1), and no mixed (3,2,+/-5/6) X/Y gauge bosons (corpus theorem in extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex)
branch_scope.reversible_repair_orientation_branch: the OPH finite patch-carrier pipeline operates on a completed compare/write/verify slice; for each unoriented adjoint generator the alphabet contains exactly two oriented primitive repair ticks (action/coaction, write/verify, ket/bra)
branch_scope.cyclic_scheduler_branch: the global repair cycle visits each oriented primitive exactly once per cycle, so the induced linear shift on the oriented support is a cyclic permutation whose order equals m_rep
branch_scope.scope_note: the representation-to-spectrum round-count theorem m_rep=24 and the specialization |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/48) are theorems on the conjunction of the three branches above; the parametric law |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/(2*m)) at the m-tick decomposition is supplied by the global repair-tick lemma.

dependency_artifacts.corpus_realized_product_branch: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (realized compact-gauge branch theorem: overlap holonomy + Tannaka reconstruction + MAR + anomaly cancellation, lines 482-504 establishing connected adjoint (8,1,0)(+)(1,3,0)(+)(1,1,0) and no mixed X/Y carrier); paper/deriving_the_particle_zoo_from_observer_consistency.tex
dependency_artifacts.corpus_orientation_doubling: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (reversible compare/write/verify slice, line 220; line 301-303: reversible write/check orientation doubles the product-adjoint repair register to 24)
dependency_artifacts.corpus_twelve_curvature_ports: extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex (line 301: the OPH screen branch supplies twelve curvature ports as the unoriented product-adjoint dimension)

dependency_acyclicity_note.summary: The bidirectional reference between R_m_rep_24_certificate.json and R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json is a peer cross-reference, not a circular dependency. The proof-level dependency graph is acyclic.

primary_theorems_are_independent.global_repair_tick_lemma_primary: R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json derives the parametric per-tick exponent law |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/(2*m)) for an m-tick homogeneous decomposition from the D6 screen-capacity normalization, readback fixed-point closure, and scale-free homogeneity. The integer m is a free parameter throughout this derivation; m_rep = 24 is not used in any step of the parametric proof.
primary_theorems_are_independent.m_rep_24_primary: This certificate derives m_rep = 24 from the OPH realized product-gauge branch + reversible orientation-doubling axiom + Lie-algebra dimension formulas + cyclic-scheduler order. Steps 1-6 of the derivation chain do not use the tick law; the per-tick exponent -1/(2m) appears in no premise or conclusion of steps 1-6.

dependency_acyclicity_note.specialized_corollary_is_a_composition_n: The specialized result |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/48) is the composition of the two independent primary theorems above. It is recorded in both R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json (as the m=m_rep=24 specialization of the parametric law) and R_m_rep_24_certificate.json derivation_chain step 7 (as the specialization at the derived m_rep=24). Recording the composition in both certs is a documentation convenience; neither primary proof feeds back into the other.
dependency_acyclicity_note.umbrella_certificate_resolves_the_compos: R_local_global_hierarchy_resonance_closeout_335.json composes both R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json and R_m_rep_24_certificate.json (and six other prerequisite theorems) as independent dependency artifacts. The umbrella is the canonical place where the -1/48 specialization is consumed without redundant cross-reference.
dependency_acyclicity_note.other_remaining_branches_are_upstream_on: The realized product-gauge branch (extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex), the reversible orientation-doubling axiom (same), the cyclic scheduler branch (recorded in branch_scope), and the source-side fixed point N_CRC^EW(P_*) (R_EW_global_capacity_certificate.json) are strictly upstream of this certificate or symbolic only; none participate in a cycle. N_CRC^EW from R_EW_global_capacity_certificate.json is referenced only symbolically in the specialized exponent (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/48); no concrete numerical value of N_CRC is used in the m_rep=24 derivation, and R_EW_global_capacity_certificate.json does not depend on this certificate.

negative_controls.name: unoriented product adjoint
negative_controls.reason: forgets the co-oriented verification half required by reversible record-preserving repair

negative_controls.name: minimal coupled carrier
negative_controls.reason: counts the matter-coupled carrier rank, not the adjoint repair spectrum

negative_controls.name: color-only doubled adjoint
negative_controls.reason: omits weak and hypercharge channels from the realized product branch

negative_controls.name: color-plus-weak doubled without U(1)
negative_controls.reason: omits the hypercharge/electromagnetic channel forced by the realized Z6 quotient

negative_controls.name: single-orientation SU(5) adjoint
negative_controls.reason: wrong support: includes the (3,2,+/-5/6) X/Y mixed gauge bosons absent on the realized OPH branch and lacks the orientation-doubling factor

negative_controls.name: doubled SU(5) adjoint
negative_controls.reason: wrong branch: contains the X/Y leptoquark carrier excluded by the OPH product-group adjoint theorem

negative_controls.name: include graviton in compact-gauge repair support
negative_controls.reason: the graviton is the dynamical metric branch and fixes the radius/capacity chart, not an internal compact-gauge adjoint repair channel

used_inputs: SU(3) adjoint dimension = 8 (compact Lie algebra dim(su(n))=n^2-1 at n=3)
SU(2) adjoint dimension = 3 (compact Lie algebra dim(su(n))=n^2-1 at n=2)
U(1) Lie algebra dimension = 1 (abelian Lie algebra)
OPH realized product-gauge branch theorem (extra/compact_proof_of_oph.tex lines 482-504)
parametric global repair-tick law |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/(2*m)) from R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json

closed_here: m_rep=2*dim(su(3)+su(2)+u(1))=24 on the realized product-gauge branch
the cyclic scheduler on the oriented adjoint support has spectral period 24
the parametric global repair-tick law specializes to |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/48) at m=24
every nearby round count is rejected on structural grounds (negative_controls)
claim_boundary.scope: the representation-to-spectrum theorem m_rep=24 and the specialization |g_*'| = (N_CRC/pi)^(-1/48) are theorems on the realized OPH compact-gauge branch + reversible orientation-doubling grammar + cyclic scheduler. The parametric per-tick law -1/(2*m) is supplied by R_N_global_repair_tick_certificate.json; the source-side fixed point N_CRC^EW(P_*) is supplied separately by R_EW_global_capacity_certificate.json.
